posts.title: Launch Day — LaerKai Goes Live
posts.excerpt: 159 prompts, 3 Reddit posts, and a dream. Day 1 of building in public.
posts.content:
Today marks the official launch of LaerKai. After weeks of collecting, testing, and organizing AI prompts, we finally went live with 159 battle-tested templates across 7 categories.

We posted to three subreddits — r/ChatGPT, r/PromptEngineering, and r/SideProject. The response was immediate.

Every prompt in the library has been tested across ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini. No fluff, no filler — just templates that actually produce good output.

The Starter Pack is free. The Pro Pack is $9.90. Let's see where this goes.
posts.author: LaerKai

posts.title: Crossing 100 Prompts
posts.excerpt: The library hit triple digits. Here's what I learned about what makes a good prompt.
posts.content:
We crossed 100 unique prompts today. Getting here taught me a lot about what separates a mediocre prompt from a great one.

The best prompts share three traits:
1. Clear role assignment — tell the AI who it is
2. Structured output format — specify exactly what you want back
3. Contextual placeholders — make customization obvious

Every prompt in LaerKai follows this pattern. That's why they work across models.
posts.author: LaerKai

posts.title: Why I Built LaerKai
posts.excerpt: I was tired of writing the same prompts over and over. So I built a library.
posts.content:
It started with frustration. Every time I needed AI to do something, I'd spend 10 minutes crafting a prompt from scratch. Then I'd do it again the next day.

So I started a text file. Then a spreadsheet. Then I realized other people had the same problem.

LaerKai is that text file, grown up. A searchable, categorized library of prompts that actually work. Free to browse, with a Pro pack for power users.

The goal: make AI 10x more useful by giving people better starting points.
posts.author: LaerKai
